# Pole and Line

Following the common information sections, the pole-and-line reporting form includes sections describing equipment, fishing operations, and catch data specific to pole-and-line fisheries.

The first section records information on the specialized equipment and machinery onboard. For pole-and-line vessels, this includes data on live bait tank capacity, the number of poles and anglers, and the types of poles and hooks used. Records in this section are linked through the Observed Trip ID.

The next section records set-level information, including the time and location of each fishing event. Subsequent subsections further characterize the setting operations, including setting cues, the number of lines fishing, and the types of bait used.

Following the set-level data, the form includes a section summarizing the total catch for the entire observed trip. Although this section appears after the set data, it is important to note that it is not recorded at the set level and is therefore identified only by the Observed Trip ID.

The next section provides catch data at a finer resolution, using both an Event ID and a Catch ID. In this section, catches are summarized by species for each fishing event. Subsequent sections record individual specimen data. These sections introduce a Specimen ID, which allows biological information to be recorded for individual fish. Data fields include length and weight measurements, sex, maturity status, sample collection details, and tag information. For Species of Special Interest (SSIs), additional fields record gear interactions, whether the individual was brought onboard, and the condition of the individual at capture and release. These sections focus on tuna and tuna-like species.

The form then includes a series of sections dedicated to baitfish data, which follow a similar structure to the tuna sections. Data are first reported for bait fishing events.  Catch summaries for baitfish species are recorded in a section that contains general catch information not linked to a specific event or trip identifier. These fields include total weight, processing type, value, and estimation methods. An additional section records Species of Special Interest (SSIs) encountered during bait fishing operations.

The final section of the pole-and-line reporting form records daily activities conducted during the observed trip, identified by the Observed Trip ID.

```{r PLStructure, fig.cap = "Structure of the Pole-and-Line reporting forms. Each sheet contains a unique identifier (UI) to identify the trip. Subsetions, each containing individual fields are mapped. Mandatory subsections are shown in blue, while optional are shown in green.", fig.asp = 0.75, width = 8}
knitr::include_graphics("../../inputs/charts/IOTC_ROS_PL.png")
```
